title: Disable unsafe dynamic execution — gate exec() behind SecurityLayer
objective: Gate all exec() calls in production code behind the SecurityLayer R3 approval gate and the NOVA_ALLOW_DYNAMIC_EXEC environment flag. In production, NOVA_ALLOW_DYNAMIC_EXEC must default to false. This removes the B102 bandit finding from the baseline.
context: P0-000 triage confirmed B102 exec_used as a Medium/High bandit finding in production code.
Confirmed affected files from bandit output:
The phase18/security/security_hardening.py SecurityLayer already provides R3 approval gating.
NOVA_ALLOW_DYNAMIC_EXEC=false is already documented in .env.example (added in P0-001).
The fix is to wire the env flag check into nova_agent.py and task_executor.py.
Any exec() call must: (1) check NOVA_ALLOW_DYNAMIC_EXEC env var, (2) require SecurityLayer R3 approval, (3) log to AuditTrail.

confirmed_bandit_findings.note: Secondary target — address if in same files

acceptance_checks: exec() in nova_agent.py is gated behind NOVA_ALLOW_DYNAMIC_EXEC env check
exec() in task_executor.py requires SecurityLayer R3 approval
NOVA_ALLOW_DYNAMIC_EXEC defaults to false when env var is not set
All exec() calls are logged to AuditTrail
bandit B102 finding is resolved for agents/ and scheduler/
New tests in tests/security/test_no_runtime_exec.py document the behavior
